mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises > 0.2pp over 3 months, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust domestic demand across consumption and investment channels, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows zero or negative predictive power for 20-day returns during expansion regimes, with out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with high inflation erode real discount rates and compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high rates and high inflation fails to predict negative returns, with model accuracy dropping below 50% in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% signals strong aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days of data release.
failure_condition: GDP releases above 3.0% growth are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or event study alpha is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect sustained labor market strength and rising household income, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal weakening residential investment and broader demand contraction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Housing start declines fail to predict negative returns during late-cycle regimes, with predictive accuracy below 50% and t-statistic < 1.5.
